current_focus.reason: BA-10-S3 closed the final required acceptance gap through the bounded maintenance workflow, retained review artifacts, and approval controls, and host-side validation has now confirmed both the unattended build wrapper and the product launchd helpers on the real machine, so the product now sits at 212 implemented / 0 partial / 0 gap scenarios with no remaining repo-tracked blockers.

validation_commands.title: Smoke harness flow
validation_commands.description: Replays the committed bootstrap -> tailoring -> discovery -> send -> feedback -> review-query smoke path.

validation_commands.title: Acceptance report guards
validation_commands.command: python3.11 -m pytest tests/test_acceptance_traceability.py tests/test_blocker_audit.py tests/test_quality_validation.py tests/test_repo_readiness.py
validation_commands.description: Keeps the committed BA-10 acceptance, blocker, readiness, and validation-suite reports synchronized with repo code, tests, and state references.

validation_commands.title: Supervisor downstream hardening regressions
validation_commands.description: Confirms incident-first selector ordering, existing-run reuse, bounded role-targeted progression through `delivery_feedback`, contact-rooted general-learning follow-through, and bounded daily maintenance selection or retention behavior.

validation_commands.title: Runtime control regressions
validation_commands.description: Covers launchd plist wiring, control commands, chat lifecycle state, delayed feedback runners, maintenance review controls, and explicit negative control cases.

validation_commands.title: Runtime pack regressions
validation_commands.description: Confirms generated runtime scaffolding stays honest about the current action catalog, maintenance workflow, and operator control surfaces.

implemented_slices.name: Runtime bootstrap and DB init skeleton
Control-state persistence and run lifecycle helpers
Paste inbox and manual capture bundle persistence
Lead refresh history and workspace snapshotting
Autonomous lead fan-out and JD recovery
JD provenance merge and mismatch review
Workspace bootstrap and step artifact scaffolding
Structured edit generation and finalize verification
Mandatory agent review and override handling
Email discovery cascade and budget tracking
Feedback event ingestion and observation windows

build_board_blockers.summary: The regenerated BA-10 trace matrix now reports 212 implemented / 0 partial / 0 gap scenarios; explicit smoke-coverage targets, implemented-slice traceability, reproducible validation-command mappings, a guarded repo-readiness summary, and a durable latest validation-suite snapshot now cover the full required acceptance surface including bounded maintenance selection, retained maintenance review artifacts, and maintenance approval controls.
build_board_blockers.impact: Required acceptance coverage is now fully traceable and replayable from committed reports; reopen this blocker only if the acceptance trace, blocker audit, repo-readiness summary, or current-focus validation suite drifts away from the zero-gap surface.
build_board_blockers.next_action: No immediate code follow-up. Keep the committed BA-10 reports current and reopen this blocker only if a new acceptance regression appears.

build_board_blockers.summary: Host-side replay now confirms `build-agent/bin/jhc-build-start` loads `gui/$UID/com.jobhuntcopilot.buildlead`, starts real build-lead heartbeat cycles, and records clean `no_work` cycle rows with the supported `codex exec` invocation after the `--ask-for-approval` incompatibility fix.
build_board_blockers.impact: The unattended build wrapper is now validated both in automated tests and on the host machine, so this blocker only needs reopening if a future host replay fails or the Codex CLI shape changes again.
build_board_blockers.next_action: No immediate follow-up. Reopen only if a future host replay fails or the supported `codex exec` contract changes.

validation_commands.title: Build-agent cycle regressions
validation_commands.description: Guards the unattended build-lead `codex exec` invocation shape so unsupported approval flags do not return.

validation_commands.title: Codex CLI compatibility check
validation_commands.description: Reconfirms the current CLI shape so unattended build wrappers do not reintroduce unsupported approval flags.

build_board_blockers.summary: Host-side replay now confirms `bin/jhc-agent-start` successfully bootstraps both `gui/$UID/com.jobhuntcopilot.supervisor` and `gui/$UID/com.jobhuntcopilot.feedback-sync`, `launchctl print` reports the expected live launchd state, and `bin/jhc-agent-stop` cleanly boots both jobs out while canonical control state returns to `stopped`.
build_board_blockers.impact: Product-side launchd startup and shutdown are now verified on the host machine, so this blocker only needs reopening if future host launchctl behavior regresses.
build_board_blockers.next_action: No immediate follow-up. Reopen only if a future host launchctl replay fails or the launchd job definitions change materially.
